description: Unauthenticated DoS via arbitrarily large password in /api/login. The login handler reads the full JSON request body without a size cap and passes the password to bcrypt, causing excessive CPU and memory consumption. Fixed in v2.63.6 by capping request bodies with http.MaxBytesReader.

vulns.description: loginHandler passes the incoming request directly to auther.Auth without limiting request body size, allowing an unauthenticated attacker to send a multi-gigabyte JSON payload.
JSONAuth decodes the entire request body into jsonCred without size validation, so an oversized password is loaded into memory before authentication.
CheckPwd is called with the user-supplied password of unbounded length. bcrypt operations scale with password length, causing CPU/memory exhaustion.
CheckPwd performs bcrypt comparison without enforcing a maximum password length, enabling unauthenticated DoS when called with a huge password.
